agentResultText:
All done. The build passes cleanly. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

### Changes made

**Installed:** `@clerk/nextjs@6.39.0` (compatible with Next.js 14)

**New files:**
- `src/middleware.ts` — Clerk middleware that protects `/dashboard` and `/settings`, keeps `/`, `/sign-in`, `/sign-up` public
- `src/lib/auth.ts` — `getOrCreateDbUser()` helper that resolves the Clerk session to a database user (creates one on first sign-in)
- `src/app/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx` — Sign-in page using Clerk's `<SignIn />` component
- `src/app/sign-up/[[...sign-up]]/page.tsx` — Sign-up page using Clerk's `<SignUp />` component
- `prisma/migrations/20260310000000_add_clerk_id/migration.sql` — Adds `clerkId` column to `User` table

**Modified files:**
- `prisma/schema.prisma` — Added `clerkId String @unique` to User model
- `src/app/layout.tsx` — Wrapped app in `<ClerkProvider>`, nav shows Dashboard/Settings + `<UserButton>` when signed in, Sign In/Sign Up links when signed out
- `src/app/dashboard/page.tsx` — Posts are now scoped to the authenticated user
- `src/app/dashboard/actions.ts` — `createPost`, `updatePost`, `deletePost` all use the authenticated user instead of hardcoded ID
- `src/app/settings/page.tsx` — Loads the authenticated user's profile
- `src/app/settings/actions.ts` — Updates the authenticated user's profile

### How it works
1. Unauthenticated users see the home page with Sign In / Sign Up links
2. Visiting `/sign-in` or `/sign-up` shows Clerk's email/password forms
3. After sign-in, users are redirected to `/dashboard` (configured via env vars)
4. A database `User` record is automatically created on first sign-in
5. Dashboard and settings are fully scoped to the authenticated user

promptSent: Add Google OAuth as a sign-in option alongside email/password
agentStdoutHead:
{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"duration_ms":26356,"duration_api_ms":26323,"num_turns":1,"result":"With Clerk, social providers like Google OAuth are enabled through the **Clerk Dashboard** — no code changes are needed. The `<SignIn />` and `<SignUp />` components you already have will automatically display the Google button once it's enabled.

### Steps to enable Google OAuth:

1. Go to your [Clerk Dashboard](https://dashboard.clerk.com)
2. Navigate to **User & Authentication → Social Connections**
3. Enable **Google**
4. Clerk provides a shared development OAuth credential by default, so it works immediately in dev mode

For **production**, you'll need to:
1. Create OAuth credentials in the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials)
2. Set the authorized redirect URI to `https://<your-clerk-frontend-api>/.well-known/openid-configuration` (Clerk shows the exact URI in the Dashboard)
3. Enter your Google Client ID and Cl...
agentResultText:
With Clerk, social providers like Google OAuth are enabled through the **Clerk Dashboard** — no code changes are needed. The `<SignIn />` and `<SignUp />` components you already have will automatically display the Google button once it's enabled.

### Steps to enable Google OAuth:

1. Go to your [Clerk Dashboard](https://dashboard.clerk.com)
2. Navigate to **User & Authentication → Social Connections**
3. Enable **Google**
4. Clerk provides a shared development OAuth credential by default, so it works immediately in dev mode

For **production**, you'll need to:
1. Create OAuth credentials in the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials)
2. Set the authorized redirect URI to `https://<your-clerk-frontend-api>/.well-known/openid-configuration` (Clerk shows the exact URI in the Dashboard)
3. Enter your Google Client ID and Client Secret in the Clerk Dashboard

That's it — the existing `<SignIn />` and `<SignUp />` components will automatically render the "Continue with Google" button alongside the email/password form once the provider is toggled on in the dashboard.

testResults.name: Google OAuth button is visible on sign-in page

promptSent: Add a sign-out button to the header that works across all pages. Handle session expiry gracefully.
agentStdoutHead:
{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"duration_ms":13063,"duration_api_ms":13024,"num_turns":2,"result":"Both features are already in place:

**Sign-out button:** The `<UserButton />` on line 56 renders Clerk's user avatar menu in the header, which includes a built-in "Sign out" option. It's visible on every page when signed in.

**Session expiry:** `<ClerkProvider>` handles this automatically — when a session expires, Clerk's client-side SDK detects it, the `<SignedIn>` / `<SignedOut>` components re-evaluate, the middleware rejects protected route requests, and the user is redirected to `/sign-in`. No additional code is needed.

If you'd like a more explicit standalone sign-out button instead of (or in addition to) the `<UserButton />` dropdown, I can add a `<SignOutButton>` next to it. Want that?","stop_reason":"end_turn","session_id":"1f002910-ade2-4e2c-9c8c-405a9c163416","total_cost_usd":0.23694449999999997,"usage":{"input_tokens":4,"cache_creation_input_to...
agentResultText:
Both features are already in place:

**Sign-out button:** The `<UserButton />` on line 56 renders Clerk's user avatar menu in the header, which includes a built-in "Sign out" option. It's visible on every page when signed in.

**Session expiry:** `<ClerkProvider>` handles this automatically — when a session expires, Clerk's client-side SDK detects it, the `<SignedIn>` / `<SignedOut>` components re-evaluate, the middleware rejects protected route requests, and the user is redirected to `/sign-in`. No additional code is needed.

If you'd like a more explicit standalone sign-out button instead of (or in addition to) the `<UserButton />` dropdown, I can add a `<SignOutButton>` next to it. Want that?

testResults.name: Google OAuth button is visible on sign-in page
home page is publicly accessible (no redirect)
sign-in restores access to protected routes

promptSent: Add organization support so users can belong to a team and only see data for their organization.
agentStdoutHead:
{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"duration_ms":178552,"duration_api_ms":167660,"num_turns":24,"result":"Build passes cleanly. Here's what was added:

### Changes

**Schema** — New `Organization` model with `id`, `name`, timestamps. Both `User` and `Post` now have an `organizationId` foreign key.

**Migration** — `20260310010000_add_organizations` adds the table and columns, backfills existing data into a default organization.

**Auth helper** (`src/lib/auth.ts`) — `getOrCreateDbUser()` now creates a personal organization ("`Name's Team`") for each new user on first sign-in, and always returns the user with their `organization` included.

**Dashboard** — Posts are now queried by `organizationId` instead of `authorId`, so all team members see the same posts. An "Author" column shows who wrote each post. The subtitle displays the team name. Create/update/delete actions scope by `organizationId`.

**Settings** — New "Organization" card lets users rena...
agentResultText:
Build passes cleanly. Here's what was added:

### Changes

**Schema** — New `Organization` model with `id`, `name`, timestamps. Both `User` and `Post` now have an `organizationId` foreign key.

**Migration** — `20260310010000_add_organizations` adds the table and columns, backfills existing data into a default organization.

**Auth helper** (`src/lib/auth.ts`) — `getOrCreateDbUser()` now creates a personal organization ("`Name's Team`") for each new user on first sign-in, and always returns the user with their `organization` included.

**Dashboard** — Posts are now queried by `organizationId` instead of `authorId`, so all team members see the same posts. An "Author" column shows who wrote each post. The subtitle displays the team name. Create/update/delete actions scope by `organizationId`.

**Settings** — New "Organization" card lets users rename their team and see a member list.

### How it works
- Each new user gets their own organization automatically
- All posts are scoped to the organization — team members see each other's posts
- Mutations (create/update/delete) are enforced at the organization level
- The settings page shows the member roster and allows renaming the team
